3.happening: Create an empty tally to hold one entry per distinct letter.
3.why: A palindrome only cares HOW MANY of each letter you have, never where they sit.
3.invariant: counts starts empty; it will end up holding every distinct letter of s.

4.why: We read the string left to right, one character at a time.
4.invariant: i is the index of the next character to count.

5.happening: Declare the holder for the character being counted.
5.why: Naming it up front means it always exists for the visual to track.
5.invariant: ch always holds the character currently under the cursor.

6.happening: {len(s) - i} character(s) still to count — keep scanning.
6.why: Every character must be tallied before we can build anything.
6.invariant: Characters before index {i} are already in counts.

6.happening: Reached the end of the string — every character is tallied.
6.why: The tally is complete, so the counting pass is done.
6.invariant: counts now holds the full frequency of every letter.

7.why: This is the letter we're about to add to the tally.
7.invariant: ch is always a single character from s.

8.happening: '{ch}' is already in the tally — we've seen it before.
8.why: A repeat sighting means we increment rather than create.
8.invariant: Each distinct letter occupies exactly one bucket.

8.happening: '{ch}' is not in the tally yet — this is its first sighting.
8.why: A brand-new letter needs a bucket created for it.
8.invariant: Each distinct letter occupies exactly one bucket.

9.why: One more copy of this letter is available to pair up.
9.invariant: counts[ch] equals how many times '{ch}' has appeared so far.

11.happening: Create the bucket for '{ch}' with a tally of 1.
11.why: First sighting — start its count at one.
11.invariant: counts[ch] equals how many times '{ch}' has appeared so far.

12.why: Move on to the next character of the string.
12.invariant: Every character before i has been counted exactly once.

13.why: We'll add characters to it pair by pair.
13.invariant: length only ever counts characters we can actually place.

14.happening: Start the odd-letter flag as False.
14.why: We don't yet know whether any letter has a leftover copy.
14.invariant: has_odd becomes True the moment any letter has an odd count.

15.happening: Collect the {len(counts)} distinct letters so we can walk them.
15.why: The second pass visits each distinct letter exactly once, not each character.
15.invariant: keys holds every letter that appears in s, once each.

16.happening: Start the second pass at the first distinct letter.
16.why: This is a fresh walk — over letters now, not over the string.
16.invariant: ki indexes into keys, not into s.

17.happening: Clear the scan character — the counting pass is over.
17.why: It should no longer highlight a bucket while we fold the tallies in.
17.invariant: ch is empty for the whole second pass.

18.happening: Declare the holder for the tally being folded in.
18.why: Each distinct letter's count is read into it in turn.
18.invariant: count always holds counts[key] for the current letter.

19.happening: {len(keys) - ki} distinct letter(s) still to fold in.
19.why: Each letter contributes however many pairs it can form.
19.invariant: length already includes every letter before index {ki}.

19.happening: Every distinct letter has been folded in.
19.why: All the pairs are accounted for; only the centre remains to decide.
19.invariant: length is now the total of all paired characters.

20.why: We fold letters in one at a time.
20.invariant: key is a letter that definitely exists in counts.

21.why: The tally decides how many mirrored pairs this letter can supply.
21.invariant: count is the true frequency of '{key}' in s.

22.why: Only PAIRS can mirror around the centre; an odd leftover can't be placed here.
22.invariant: length counts only characters that can be mirrored.

23.happening: '{key}' has an odd count ({count}) — one copy is left over.
23.why: A leftover copy is a candidate for the single centre slot.
23.invariant: At most one leftover character can ever be used.

23.happening: '{key}' has an even count ({count}) — every copy is paired.
23.why: Nothing is left over, so this letter can't supply a centre.
23.invariant: At most one leftover character can ever be used.

24.happening: Record that a leftover letter exists.
24.why: It doesn't matter WHICH letter sits in the centre — only that one can.
24.invariant: has_odd stays True once set; we never need to count leftovers.

25.happening: Move to the next distinct letter.
25.why: Every letter must be folded in before we finish.
25.invariant: Letters before index {ki} are fully accounted for.

26.happening: At least one letter had a leftover — we can place a centre character.
26.why: A palindrome of odd length has exactly one unmirrored character in the middle.
26.invariant: The centre adds exactly 1, no matter how many leftovers there were.

26.happening: Every letter paired up perfectly — no centre character is available.
26.why: With no leftovers the palindrome must be even length.

27.why: One spare letter can sit in the middle without needing a mirror.
27.invariant: Only ever +1, even if many letters had odd counts.

28.happening: The longest palindrome we can build has length {length}.
28.why: All pairs, plus one centre if a leftover existed.
28.invariant: No arrangement of these letters can beat this length.

init.happening: Setting up an empty tally and the scan cursor.
init.why: We need to know how many of each letter we have before building anything.

loop.happening: Walking — first over the string's characters, then over the distinct letters.
loop.why: One pass to count, one pass to convert counts into pairs.
loop.invariant: Each pass visits every item exactly once.

check.happening: Testing a condition — is this letter known, is its count odd, was there any leftover?
check.why: The branches decide whether to create or increment, and whether a centre exists.
check.invariant: No state changes on a check line.

update.happening: Recording a tally or adding paired characters to the answer.
update.why: The tally and the running length are the only state that matters.
update.invariant: length only ever grows by whole pairs, plus one final centre.

move.happening: Advancing a cursor to the next character or the next distinct letter.
move.why: Both passes march forward one item at a time.

return.happening: Returning the longest palindrome length that can be built.
return.why: Every pair is counted and the centre is decided.
return.invariant: The answer is the final value of length.
